title: Technology in Our Daily Lives
objectives: Select appropriate expressions to agree, disagree, or give advice about daily technology use
Express personal viewpoints about technology use clearly using learned phrases

key_concepts.explanation: Agreement: I agree because…, That's true. Disagreement: I don't agree because…, I'm not sure about that. Giving Advice: You should…, You shouldn't…, It's better to…

A: I use my phone every day to check homework and chat with friends. B: Me too! I do the same, but I think people should spend less time online. A: I agree. We should take breaks and do other things too. This dialogue shows: talking about technology use, identifying a problem (too much time online), showing agreement, and giving advice.
Topics for discussion: phones, social media, video games, smart homes. For each: one benefit, one problem it can cause, one piece of advice for using it responsibly.

exercises.problem: Look at the pictures of different inventions. What device can you see in each picture? What is each person using it for? Do you or your family use a similar device? When?
exercises.answer: Open discussion about technology in daily life

exercises.problem: Read the dialogue. Answer: 1. What technology are they talking about? 2. Why do they use it? 3. What problem do they mention? 4. Which sentence shows agreement/disagreement? 5. Which sentence gives advice?
exercises.answer: 1. Phone. 2. Homework and chatting. 3. Spending too much time online. 4. 'I agree' (agreement). 5. 'We should take breaks and do other things too.' (advice).

exercises.problem: My Tech Habits: In pairs, talk about how you use technology every day. Describe how, when, and why. Listen to your partner and say whether you agree or disagree. Give one piece of advice about using that device wisely.
exercises.answer: Example: A: I use my tablet to watch videos after school because it helps me relax. B: That's nice, but you shouldn't watch for too long.

exercises.problem: Smart Use of Technology: In groups, choose one topic (phones, social media, video games, smart homes). Discuss: one benefit, one problem, one piece of advice for responsible use. Use linkers and speaking phrases. Share with the class.
exercises.answer: Open-ended group discussion with Speaking Checklist: I can describe how I use technology, I can agree and disagree politely, I used linkers to connect ideas, I can listen carefully and respond clearly.

teaching_notes: Speaking lesson on technology in daily life. Note: page title says 'Everyday Role Models' but content is about technology use — likely a printing error in the textbook. Model dialogue demonstrates agreement/disagreement/advice. Useful Language box with phrases for each. Linker Bank continues. Speaking Tips: speak clearly, use short sentences, listen carefully, don't interrupt, be polite when you disagree. Speaking Checklist for self-assessment. 1 page.
